experiment: prospective cheap-tier outcome-equivalence on mechanical turns
design: Each mechanical task was run by two independent blind subagents over the same real repositories: a CHEAP tier and a PREMIUM tier. Neither agent saw the answer; each had to perform the tool orchestration (ls/grep/read/json) itself. Ground truth was computed deterministically beforehand. Outcome-equivalence = cheap-tier answer equals premium-tier answer equals ground truth.
note_on_reproducibility: Ground truth is recomputed from source files pinned by SHA-256 plus a public loopforge revision. The local AgentGuard eval inputs are not redistributed. Per-tier answers are recorded blind runs with the exact prompt.

tasks.prompt: count files matching eval/*.py in agentguard
count top-level def lines in eval/trifecta_reach.py
count rows with trifecta_complete=true in marketplace results json
count total rows in marketplace results json

routing_decision: No automatic route change: the refreshed premium run missed T1, so full outcome equivalence was not reproduced.
limitations: The local AgentGuard eval inputs are identified by hashes but are not published in this repository.
This six-task pilot is too small to establish broad model-tier equivalence.
